title: sm-max-tokens-truncation-repeat
description:
This is a continuation of sm-max-tokens-truncation-repeat. In the current window, the sm persona again produced two consecutive failed runs with explicit truncation signatures at 2026-05-29T13:32:21.682850+00:00 and 2026-05-29T13:32:21.700504+00:00. Both failures self-identify as JSON parse errors caused by finish_reason=length at max_tokens, matching the same factory-side output truncation pattern seen in multiple immediately preceding concerns.

The pattern remains narrow and diagnostically clear: the failures are isolated to sm, occur back-to-back, and include error text that names the mechanism of failure rather than requiring inference. In this specific window the measurable direct cost recorded on the failed runs is 0.0 USD and the failures resolve immediately into subsequent successful sm and reviewer runs, so there is no evidence here of a full queue stall. However, the repeated recurrence across consecutive watcher intervals indicates the condition has not resolved and is likely to keep reappearing.

Other failures in the window are fast sandbox-style "tests not green after run" events across onboarder, test_implementer, and dev, but those are separate from the sm truncation signature and do not by themselves establish a coherent new factory-wide incident here. Queue, tick, spend, and placeholder signals were quiet or inconclusive in this interval.

Because this same clearly attributable truncation class has now persisted across multiple watcher-note intervals without improvement, it merits continued investigation by L3 even though this single window does not show severe downstream harm on its own.

evidence.summary_excerpt: This window was active with another burst of fast sandbox-style failures reporting "tests not green after run" across onboarder, test_implementer, and dev, plus two more sm JSON parse failures caused by truncation at max_tokens with finish_reason=length.

evidence.excerpt: persona=sm success=false error="json parse failed at max_tokens=65536 finish_reason=length: Unterminated string starting at: line 1 column 9 (char 8)" tokens_out=122880 model=deepseek/deepseek-chat attempt_n=832

persona=sm success=false error="json parse failed at max_tokens=8192 finish_reason=length: Unterminated string starting at: line 1 column 7 (char 6)" tokens_out=20 model=deepseek/deepseek-chat attempt_n=833
persona=sm success=true model=deepseek/deepseek-chat attempt_n=834 tokens_in=10 tokens_out=20

{"noteworthy":"Seven failed runs were seen: a repeated cluster of sandbox-style \"tests not green after run\" failures across onboarder, test_implementer, and dev, plus two sm failures whose error text explicitly diagnoses truncation at max_tokens with finish_reason=length."}
{"noteworthy":"onboarder reached failure_count=3 in this window, which meets the retry-storm heuristic; sm also failed twice in quick succession with the same truncation pattern."}

escalation_reason: Repeated sm max-tokens truncation failures have persisted across multiple consecutive watcher intervals through 2026-05-29T13:32:36.099489+00:00, with the same explicit finish_reason=length JSON-parse signature recurring again in two back-to-back runs. L3 judgment is needed because this is a clear, factory-attributable model/output-cap pattern that has not resolved after repeated recurrence.
